decision_rules.if: Endorsement 4A present AND prior loss history AND panel-cause signature
decision_rules.then: Issue ROR letter; call insured to explain before letter arrives

decision_rules.if: First 10 days of a large fire claim
decision_rules.then: Hold ALE conservative; commit only to two weeks initially

decision_rules.if: Origin report identifies specific panel model in active recall
decision_rules.then: Open subrogation and preserve the panel as evidence

decision_rules.if: Origin report clears panel-cause with no manufacturer defect
decision_rules.then: Release ROR the same day; call insured before letter

decision_rules.if: Reserve setting on a novel-pattern fire
decision_rules.then: Set to median outcome for the pattern; mark review Day 10 and Day 30

exception_patterns.pattern: Cause reads as panel but is actually branch wiring
exception_patterns.resolution: Wait for fire marshal breakdown before locking reserve; expect 30–40% reserve upward revision

exception_patterns.pattern: Prior loss present but from unrelated cause family (e.g. water)
exception_patterns.resolution: Do not automatically ROR; treat as clean panel-cause absent other signals
Still preserve the panel; recall subro still available even without 4A dwelling extension

heuristics.text: The tell for me is always three things stacked — 4A, prior loss, and something in the first-notice that reads like a panel cause.
heuristics.invoked_when: FNOL review for large fire claims

heuristics.text: You reserve for what you know, not what you fear.
Preserve the panel. Always. Even in the small fires.
heuristics.invoked_when: Any panel-cause fire regardless of reserve size

heuristics.text: The panel manufacturers have been on notice for a decade.
heuristics.invoked_when: Origin report calls out a specific panel model

heuristics.text: That's how you keep a customer for life — call before the letter, call the day it clears.
